skill.name: nature-reader
skill.description: Build full-paper Chinese-English side-by-side, figure/table-aware, source-grounded Markdown readers for journal or conference papers from PDF, DOI, arXiv, publisher HTML, or pasted text. Use whenever the user asks to translate or read a paper, make 中英文对照/原文对照/全文翻译解读, extract figures or tables into the right positions, preserve figure/table placement near relevant prose, or keep exact source anchors for every block. This skill must not degrade into a summary-only output unless the user explicitly asks for a summary. Also trigger on general paper-reading and translation requests even without the word "Nature", such as reading/translating an academic paper, literature reading, understanding a paper, and Chinese phrasings like 读论文、精读论文、论文翻译、文献翻译、文献阅读、学术阅读、帮我读这篇文章、翻译这篇paper.
skill.summary: Convert academic papers into bilingual Chinese-English Markdown readers with figure/table extraction and source-grounded traceability.
skill.author: Community contribution, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: The nature-reader skill is a documentation-only skill consisting entirely of Markdown instruction files and a YAML manifest. There is no executable code, no network requests, no scripts, and no environment access. All 170 static findings are false positives: the 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are Markdown inline code formatting, 'path traversal' patterns are relative cross-reference paths in documentation, 'weak cryptographic' findings are block ID naming conventions, and 'system reconnaissance' findings are file structure descriptions. No security risk was identified.

low_findings.title: False positive: Backtick patterns in Markdown documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 147 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across Markdown files. These are all Markdown inline code spans (e.g., `paper.md`, `source_map.json`, `pdf-text`, `assets/`) used for formatting file references and identifiers in documentation. No actual code execution occurs. Pattern matching on Markdown syntax without context-awareness produces these false positives.

low_findings.title: False positive: Relative path references in documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 'path traversal' at manifest.yaml:11 and other locations. These are standard relative path references for cross-file documentation linking (e.g., `../_shared/core/terminology-ledger.md`). No actual filesystem traversal or injection is possible since these are documentation strings read by the AI, not executed.

content.user_title: Read and translate academic papers bilingually
content.value_statement: Reading academic papers in a foreign language is slow and imprecise. This skill converts PDFs, DOIs, arXiv papers, and HTML articles into complete bilingual Markdown readers with figures, tables, and source-grounded citations.
actual_capabilities: Convert academic papers into paragraph-level bilingual Markdown readers with original text and Chinese translation side by side
Extract figures and tables from PDFs and place them near their first substantive mention in the translation
Maintain stable source block IDs (S001, F001, T001) for every paragraph, figure, and table for traceability
Support multiple input formats: PDF text, scanned PDFs, publisher HTML, DOI/arXiv links, and pasted text
Generate output files including paper.md, source_map.json, translation_notes.md, and an assets folder
Preserve technical terms, equations, citations, and numeric values during translation
limitations: Cannot reproduce large copyrighted publisher text directly in chat responses — must be saved to local artifact files
OCR quality for scanned PDFs depends on the source document clarity; uncertain blocks are marked rather than guessed
Requires the full document to produce a complete reader; will not summarize unless explicitly requested
Translation quality depends on the underlying language model capabilities

use_cases.title: Graduate student reading a Chinese-language paper
use_cases.description: A graduate student needs to understand a Chinese academic paper and wants bilingual output to verify translation accuracy against the original.

use_cases.title: Researcher translating a paper for a colleague
use_cases.description: A researcher wants to share a translated version of an important paper with a colleague who reads Chinese, maintaining figure positions and citations.

use_cases.title: Literature review assistant
use_cases.description: A PhD candidate conducting literature review needs to process multiple papers efficiently while maintaining traceability back to source blocks for follow-up questions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF translation
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate this paper into a bilingual reader: [paste PDF or attach file]. Keep figures and tables near their discussion sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a selectable-text PDF and wants a complete bilingual reader

prompt_templates.title: DOI or arXiv resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a bilingual reader for this paper: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-2024-12345 or arXiv:2401.12345. Resolve the link first, then translate.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a DOI or arXiv identifier that needs resolution before processing

prompt_templates.title: Pasted text processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is pasted text from a paper [paste content]. Create a bilingual Markdown reader with stable block IDs for each paragraph.
prompt_templates.scenario: User pastes prose without a retrievable original layout

prompt_templates.title: Follow-up question with source grounding
prompt_templates.prompt: Looking at the paper.md you created, what does Section 3 say about the model's training data? Cite the block ID and page number.
prompt_templates.scenario: User asks follow-up questions that require source-grounded answers

output_examples.input: A user provides a PDF of a machine learning paper and asks for a bilingual reader
output: A paper.md file containing the full paper with original English and Chinese translation in paired blocks
Stable block anchors like S001, S002 for traceability
Figures and tables extracted to assets/ folder and placed near first mention
A terminology table for recurring technical terms
A source_map.json file mapping every block to its page number and translation status

output_examples.input: A user provides an arXiv link and asks for translation
output: Resolution step converts the arXiv ID to a PDF URL
The PDF text is then processed through the standard bilingual reader pipeline
Final output includes paper.md with full bilingual content and asset folder with extracted figures

best_practices: Always process the full document when a complete paper is provided — do not stop at the abstract or introduction
State the detected source_format (pdf-text, scanned-pdf, html, doi-arxiv, or pasted-text) before processing so users can correct early
Mark uncertain blocks in translation_notes.md rather than guessing when OCR or layout extraction is weak
Keep the Terminology Ledger consistent across the whole document for recurring technical terms
anti_patterns: Do not degrade a translation request into a summary-only output unless the user explicitly asks for a summary
Do not convert dense prose into bullet-point keywords — preserve paragraph form
Do not silently skip Methods, limitations, data availability, or competing interests sections
Do not reproduce large copyrighted text directly in chat — save to local artifact files

faq.question: What input formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports selectable-text PDFs, scanned/image-only PDFs, publisher HTML pages, DOI or arXiv identifiers, and pasted text.

faq.question: Does this skill summarize papers?
faq.answer: No. The default output is a complete bilingual reader, not a summary. Use a summarization skill if you only want a summary.

faq.question: How are figures and tables handled?
faq.answer: Figures and tables are extracted as image assets and placed near their first substantive mention in the translation, with both original and translated captions.

faq.question: What output files are generated?
faq.answer: The skill generates paper.md (main bilingual reader), source_map.json (block traceability), translation_notes.md (uncertainty notes), and an assets/ folder with extracted figures and tables.

faq.question: Can I ask follow-up questions after the reader is created?
faq.answer: Yes. Follow-up questions are answered from the paper itself, with citations to specific block IDs and page numbers for full source grounding.

faq.question: Is this skill limited to Nature journal papers?
faq.answer: No. Despite the name, it works on any academic paper, preprint, or conference proceeding across disciplines.
